title: Smoky, Gooey, Show-off Saudi-Arabian Mustard Dumplings
description: A smoky, gooey, show-off dish from Saudi Arabia made with wholegrain mustard, dum, and spices, perfect for a pescatarian-friendly meal served in a kadhai. Serve with a dollop of yogurt or a side of rice for a flavorful meal.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine mustard, ginger, garlic, flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper. Blend until smooth.
Heat oil in a kadhai over medium-high heat.
Add ground lamb and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the blended mustard mixture and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes.
Add chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes.
While the dumplings are cooking, prepare the yogurt sauce by mixing yogurt with a pinch of salt and a sprinkle of black pepper.
To serve, place dumplings in a bowl, dollop with yogurt sauce, and garnish with chopped cilantro or scallions if desired.
